summary.intent: Address a Codex automated review of PR #680 on the ars-ui repo, specifically fixing 7 unresolved threads about slider component edge-case consistency around 'effective bounded value' and hidden input disabled behavior.
summary.outcome: All 7 Codex review threads were resolved by patching the slider component to normalize bounds and route all output surfaces (ARIA, form value, geometry, markers) through an effective bounded value, adding disabled attribute to hidden inputs, updating the spec, and pushing a third review-fix commit that passed the full cargo xci-fast gate.

repo: The repo uses a Codex bot (@codex review) for automated PR review; it signals acknowledgment with 👀 and approval with 👍 reactions on the PR issue.
The CI gate is `cargo xci-fast` which runs 10 gates: fmt, check, clippy, unit tests, integration tests, and adapter compilation.
Snapshot testing uses `cargo insta test` with `--unreferenced=reject --accept` flags; snapshots must be accepted after changes.
The spec lives at `spec/components/input/slider.md` and has a corresponding corpus snippet compile test (`cargo test -p xtask --test spec_corpus_compile_snippets`).
The project uses `cargo xtask spec validate` for spec validation as part of the pre-push workflow.
Locale/number formatting for slider value text comes from `crates/ars-i18n/src/number/mod.rs`.
The slider component lives in `crates/ars-components/src/input/slider/mod.rs` and is a large file (2400+ lines).

code.finding: ARIA attributes (aria-valuenow, aria-valuemin, aria-valuemax, aria-valuetext) and form hidden input were not consistently using a normalized 'effective bounded value'; patched to route all output surfaces through the same helper.
Geometry (value_percent) and marker in-range logic needed to use the same bounded/clamped value as ARIA and form output for consistency.
Hidden input was missing the native `disabled` attribute when the slider is disabled; the form submission behavior was inconsistent with ARIA disabled state.
Spec was updated to explicitly document the normalized/effective value contract for ARIA, form output, geometry, and markers to keep spec and implementation aligned.

workflow: The workflow pattern is: write failing tests → run to confirm failures → patch implementation → run tests to confirm green → update spec → format → refresh snapshots → run focused lint/spec checks → run full gate → commit and push.
Codex review polling was done via a shell loop checking GitHub API for reaction changes and unresolved thread counts, sleeping 120s between checks.
After pushing snapshot-changing commits, the `snapshot-reviewed` label must be reapplied to the PR.
Review threads should be replied to inline with the fix commit reference before resolving, then request a new `@codex review` pass.
Commit co-author trailers are used: `Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>` for review-fix commits.

friction: Codex review took an extended polling period (many 2-minute intervals) before returning the 7 new threads, requiring repeated wait cycles.
Patch application (`apply_patch`) failed on the first attempt due to context mismatch, requiring the tests to be applied in smaller chunks.
The snapshot test run (`cargo insta test`) required multiple write_stdin interactions to navigate prompts before completing.
The cargo xci-fast gate is slow (multiple stages including adapter compilation), requiring long waits before being able to push.
open_items: The CI unit tests were still running when the direct PR status was checked mid-session; full CI green status was not confirmed before the final push.
Only the enabled hidden-input form case is covered by existing snapshots; a disabled hidden-input snapshot case may be worth adding for regression coverage.

The locale-aware discrete fallback text path in the number formatter (`crates/ars-i18n/src/number/mod.rs`) was investigated but the extent of test coverage for locale-specific slider value text formatting is unclear.
